title: Check verdict — check-verdict.json (v2)
description: Machine-readable verdict sidecar emitted by /check synthesis (and, in v2, by /spec-review and /plan synthesis with the same shape). Written to docs/specs/<slug>/check-verdict.json (or spec-review-verdict.json / plan-verdict.json — discriminated by the `stage` field) by the deterministic post-processor in check.sh after extracting the sole ```check-verdict fenced JSON block from the synthesis stdout stream. Drives autorun's GO/GO_WITH_FIXES/NO_GO branching and security-finding integrity gates. v6 of autorun-overnight-policy intentionally omits any nonce mechanism (see check v3 NO-GO). v2 (pipeline-gate-permissiveness) adds per-axis policy fields: iteration counter, mode + mode_source for permissive/strict resolution, class_breakdown for post-Judge classification stats, class_inferred_count for coerced-to-unclassified findings, followups_file pointer, cap_reached flag, and stage discriminator. Consumers reading historical v1 verdicts must branch on schema_version.

schema_version.description: Bumped on any breaking schema change. v2 adds per-axis policy fields for pipeline-gate-permissiveness; consumers reading historical v1 verdicts must branch on schema_version.

prompt_version.description: Identifier of the check-verdict synthesis prompt revision that produced this sidecar. Bumped when the synthesis contract or schema changes.

verdict.description: Overall gate verdict. GO = ship as-is; GO_WITH_FIXES = ship after addressing non-blocking findings; NO_GO = blocking findings present, do not proceed to /build.

blocking_findings.description: Findings that block the gate. Empty array iff verdict is GO. May overlap with security_findings (a security blocker appears in both).

persona.description: Persona name that surfaced this finding (e.g. 'security', 'codex-adversary').

finding_id.description: ck-<first 10 hex chars of sha256(normalized_signature)>. Stage prefix 'ck' = check. Matches the persona-metrics convention in schemas/findings.schema.json.

summary.description: Short headline of the finding for autorun fix-attempt routing and PR body summarization.

security_findings.description: Findings tagged 'sev:security'. Hardcoded as blocking by autorun integrity policy regardless of policies.security_findings config. Empty array if none.

persona.description: Persona name that surfaced this finding.

finding_id.description: ck-<first 10 hex chars of sha256(normalized_signature)>. Same derivation as blocking_findings.finding_id.

summary.description: Short headline of the security concern.

tag.description: Literal tag identifying this row as security-class. Pinned const so the integrity post-processor can match without ambiguity.

generated_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp when synthesis emitted the verdict (e.g. '2026-05-05T18:42:11Z'). Used for staleness checks and audit trail.

iteration.description: 1-indexed iteration counter; increments past iteration_max only when cap reached

iteration_max.description: = gate_max_recycles from spec frontmatter; clamped to 5

class_breakdown.description: Counts of post-Judge findings by class. All 7 class keys required even when zero — fixed-shape contract simplifies consumers.

class_inferred_count.description: Findings coerced to unclassified at Judge step due to missing/invalid class field

followups_file.description: Path to docs/specs/<feature>/followups.jsonl; null iff that file does not exist on disk

cap_reached.description: True iff iteration > iteration_max — auto-promotion fired this gate run
